certification_rule: If any hypothesis is implemented as a primitive dispatch from scalar sign, label, or expected Delta L, certification must be blocked.

hypotheses.name: opposite_sign_center_no_gradient_hypothesis
hypotheses.proof_obligation: Must be derived from generated scalar signs, association records, and path-center gradients without reading a target Delta L.
hypotheses.statement: Opposite scalar-sign support pairs produce a center inflection/no-gradient invalidity.

hypotheses.name: same_sign_center_ambiguous_gradient_hypothesis
hypotheses.proof_obligation: Must be derived from generated scalar signs, association records, and path-center gradients without reading a target Delta L.
hypotheses.statement: Same scalar-sign support pairs produce a center peak/ambiguous-gradient invalidity.

hypotheses.name: no_gradient_removal_accommodation_hypothesis
hypotheses.proof_obligation: Must be justified as a topology transaction policy after center invalidity is observed; it must not be a sign-label dispatch rule.
hypotheses.statement: A no-gradient invalid relational carrier is accommodated by removal.

hypotheses.name: ambiguous_gradient_vacuum_insertion_hypothesis
hypotheses.proof_obligation: Must be justified as a topology transaction policy after center invalidity is observed; it must not be a sign-label dispatch rule.
hypotheses.statement: An ambiguous-gradient invalid center is accommodated by insertion of a vacuum point.

hypotheses.name: dangling_vacuum_reassociation_hypothesis
hypotheses.proof_obligation: Must be audited as a consequence of an admitted transaction rather than as a preselected path outcome.
hypotheses.statement: Removal leaves dangling beside-path associations that reassociate to vacuum; insertion creates new vacuum associations on both sides of the path.
